prerequisites: A Statuspage account with access to the management interface at https://manage.statuspage.io.
At least one Statuspage page (the API is organized around a page_id, so an active page is needed to return meaningful data).
A Statuspage API key generated from the User menu > API info in the management interface.
Network access to the Statuspage API base URL https://api.statuspage.io/v1/.

authMethods.name: API key (Authorization: OAuth header)
authMethods.summary: Statuspage authenticates with a single account API key. Provide it in the connector's api_key field. pm sends it to the Statuspage API in the Authorization header using the format 'Authorization: OAuth <api_key>'. Each API token is limited to 1 request per second measured over a 60 second rolling window; exceeding it returns HTTP 420 or 429.

setupSteps.title: Generate a Statuspage API key
setupSteps.body: Log in to the Statuspage management interface at https://manage.statuspage.io/login. Click your avatar in the bottom left of the screen to open the user menu, then click 'API info'. Copy the API key shown on that page. This key is scoped to your Statuspage account and is the only credential the connector needs.

setupSteps.title: Provide the api_key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Set the required api_key field to the value copied from the API info page. pm uses this key to authenticate against the Statuspage API at https://api.statuspage.io/v1/, sending it as 'Authorization: OAuth <api_key>'. Treat the key as a secret; anyone with it can read and modify your Statuspage data.

setupSteps.title: Verify connectivity
setupSteps.body: Confirm the key works by listing your pages, e.g. curl -H "Authorization: OAuth <api_key>" https://api.statuspage.io/v1/pages. A successful JSON response with your page(s) confirms the credential is valid. Keep request volume under the 1 request/second per-token rate limit to avoid 420/429 errors.
